description: Machine-readable dictionary of SoundSpectrAnalyse compiled metrics. Each entry documents the metric's status (canonical / diagnostic / legacy), the formula, the denominator, the physical quantity type, the metric family, the upstream metrics it derives from, whether it is independent for PCA, and how the value MUST and MUST NOT be interpreted. Consumers should treat 'canonical' as final scientific outputs and isolate 'legacy' to back-compat use only.
pca_inclusion_policy: PCA exports must exclude every entry with independent_for_pca=false unless an explicit debug flag is passed. The 'derived_from' field documents the algebraic dependencies that motivate this exclusion.

duplicate_resolution_v1_1: harmonic_energy_ratio, inharmonic_energy_ratio, subbass_energy_ratio share the same H/(H+I+S) formula as the component_* names. They are demoted to status='diagnostic' (alias of component_*).
component_subbass_energy_ratio is the algebraic complement of component_harmonic_energy_ratio + component_inharmonic_energy_ratio (sum = 1). It is canonical but independent_for_pca=false.
component_total_inharmonic_energy_ratio is the exact sum (I+S)/(H+I+S). Canonical, but independent_for_pca=false.

effective_partial_count and effective_partial_density are NOT identical: effective_partial_count is N_eff over harmonic peaks only (computed in proc_audio); effective_partial_density is N_eff over the harmonic + aggregated inharmonic + sub-bass bundle (density.partial_density_effective_components_bundle). Both are canonical with distinct inputs.

density_normalized_global vs density_metric_normalized (v1.2): previous schema versions treated density_metric_normalized as a compatibility alias of density_normalized_global. As of the single-pass weighted-density refactor they are distinct descriptors: density_normalized_global is the canonical max-norm of canonical_density_v5_adapted (publication-grade, lives on Canonical_Metrics); density_metric_normalized is the run-relative max-norm of density_metric_raw (the weighted partial-sum density, lives on Density_Metrics) and is classified as diagnostic.

v1.3 (inharmonic-energy underestimation audit): the legacy linear_amplitude_batch_alignment_factor that rescaled the exported Inharmonic Spectrum + Sub-bass band amplitudes towards a batch energy budget (default harmonic_weight=0.95) is now disabled by default in integrated_single_pass mode (export_alignment_factor = 1.0, export_alignment_source = 'disabled_integrated_single_pass'). It is still computed in explicit legacy_batch mode but the scaled values are written to a new Amplitude_display_scaled column instead of overwriting Amplitude; the raw amplitudes are now always exported as Amplitude_raw and Power_raw = Amplitude_raw² so the compiled Density_Metrics extraction can prefer raw inputs.

v1.3: harmonic-slot acceptance tightened — a harmonic order is only accepted when there is a validated local peak inside the tolerance window (local maximum, ≥ 3 dB prominence above neighbours, SNR ≥ 3 dB above local noise floor); the previous silent 'nearest-bin' fallbacks that promoted orders without a validated peak are removed. Orders without a validated candidate are now left missing and their energy is correctly attributed to the inharmonic / residual buckets.

v1.3 new component_energy entries: component_residual_noise_energy_ratio (diffuse non-harmonic energy outside accepted harmonic / inharmonic / sub-bass masks; denominator H+I+S+residual; status=diagnostic, independent_for_pca=true) and component_nonharmonic_energy_ratio (1 − H/(H+I+S+residual); status=diagnostic, independent_for_pca=false because it is a derived complement). component_inharmonic_energy_ratio retains its H+I+S denominator semantics — the new residual ratio captures broadband bow / breath noise that the discrete-inharmonic accounting alone misses.

v1.3.2 (May 2026): proc_audio exports per-note Legacy_Density_Metrics sheet (SDM, FDM, CDM, Density Metric) by default; compile read_excel_metrics merges it for Weighted Combined Metric. Research export adds density_weighted_sum_cdm_mean = (density_weighted_sum + Combined Density Metric) / 2 on compiled_density_metrics_research.xlsx only — editorial, not canonical.

metrics.name: Note
metrics.formula: row key extracted from filename (e.g., 'A4')
metrics.interpretation: identifies the musical note this row pertains to.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a scientific result; this is an identifier.

metrics.name: source_file_name
metrics.formula: absolute or relative path to the analysed WAV
metrics.interpretation: identifies the source audio file.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a measurement.

metrics.name: tier
metrics.formula: tier name assigned from FFT_SETTINGS_BY_CLUSTER by fundamental frequency
metrics.interpretation: documents the frequency-tier-specific STFT parameters (n_fft, hop_length, tolerance) used.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a quality grade of the analysis.

metrics.name: component_harmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: H / (H + I + S)
metrics.interpretation: fraction of the analysed component power that is held by detected harmonic partials.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: loudness, perceptual prominence, or a fraction of total signal energy (the denominator excludes residual full-spectrum noise outside the analysed component set).

metrics.name: component_inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: I / (H + I + S)
metrics.interpretation: fraction of the analysed component power held by detected inharmonic (non-integer-multiple) partials.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: perceptual roughness or dissonance; those are separate models.

metrics.name: component_subbass_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: S / (H + I + S)
metrics.interpretation: fraction of the analysed component power held by the aggregated sub-bass / ground-noise band below the configured cut-off (default ~200 Hz).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a separate noise-floor estimate; this aggregator includes any sub-bass peaks excluded from the harmonic template.

metrics.name: component_total_inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.interpretation: convenience sum of the inharmonic and sub-bass component shares; non-harmonic share of analysed components.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a noise-to-signal ratio; this excludes content outside the analysed peak / sub-bass aggregation.

metrics.name: component_residual_noise_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: residual / (H + I + S + residual), residual = max(0, ΣA²|filtered − (H + I + S))
metrics.denominator: H + I + S + residual (total surviving spectral energy after noise-floor rejection)
metrics.interpretation: diffuse non-harmonic residual energy fraction; spectral power that survived the noise-floor rejection but is NOT in the accepted harmonic peaks, NOT in the discrete inharmonic peaks, and NOT in the sub-bass region. Captures broadband bow/breath noise that the discrete-inharmonic accounting alone would miss.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the same quantity as component_inharmonic_energy_ratio (denominator is intentionally different — H+I+S+residual vs H+I+S — and the masks are disjoint).

metrics.name: component_nonharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: (I + S + residual) / (H + I + S + residual) = 1 − H / (H + I + S + residual)
metrics.denominator: H + I + S + residual (total surviving spectral energy after noise-floor rejection)
metrics.interpretation: total non-harmonic share of the surviving spectrum: discrete inharmonic + sub-bass + diffuse residual. Complementary to component_harmonic_energy_ratio computed over the extended denominator.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the arithmetic sum of component_inharmonic_energy_ratio + component_subbass_energy_ratio + component_residual_noise_energy_ratio when those use different denominators (this metric uses H+I+S+residual throughout).

metrics.name: model_harmonic_weight
metrics.formula: H / (H + I)
metrics.denominator: H + I (binary partition, EXCLUDES sub-bass on purpose)
metrics.interpretation: binary coefficient used by downstream weighting / dissonance models to blend harmonic and inharmonic contributions.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the same number as component_harmonic_energy_ratio; the denominator is intentionally H + I, not H + I + S.

metrics.name: model_inharmonic_weight
metrics.formula: I / (H + I)
metrics.denominator: H + I (binary partition, EXCLUDES sub-bass on purpose)
metrics.interpretation: binary coefficient used by downstream weighting / dissonance models.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the same number as component_inharmonic_energy_ratio; the denominator is intentionally H + I, not H + I + S.

metrics.name: effective_partial_count
metrics.formula: N_eff = (Σ P_h_i)^2 / Σ (P_h_i)^2  with P_h_i = A_h_i^2 over HARMONIC peaks only
metrics.denominator: n/a (Hill q=2 / inverse Herfindahl over harmonic peaks)
metrics.unit: dimensionless effective count of harmonic partials
metrics.interpretation: effective number of energetically participating HARMONIC partials. ≈1 for a single dominant harmonic; ≈N for N equal-energy harmonics.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the same quantity as effective_partial_density (which is a BLENDED N_eff over harmonic + inharmonic + sub-bass). Inputs differ.

metrics.name: effective_partial_density
metrics.formula: participation-ratio style effective number of energetically relevant partials over the BLENDED bundle (harmonic + aggregated inharmonic + sub-bass aggregate)
metrics.interpretation: density / fatness descriptor over the blended bundle: how many partials carry the bulk of the H+I+S energy.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the same as effective_partial_count; effective_partial_count restricts to harmonic peaks only.

metrics.name: canonical_density_v5_adapted
metrics.formula: canonical density v5 with effective partial weighting (see density.CANONICAL_DENSITY_FORMULA_VERSION)
metrics.interpretation: current published density metric for SoundSpectrAnalyse.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: comparable across different weight_function settings; verify density_source_formula / density_formula_version.

metrics.name: canonical_density
metrics.formula: Publication alias: identical scalar to canonical_density_v5_adapted (same pipeline; see density.CANONICAL_DENSITY_FORMULA_VERSION).
metrics.interpretation: Publication-facing column name for the canonical v5 adapted density; use when the internal lineage suffix is not needed in tables.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent quantity from canonical_density_v5_adapted; values are duplicated for readability only.

metrics.name: density_normalized_global
metrics.formula: canonical_density_v5_adapted / max(canonical_density_v5_adapted) over the compiled workbook (clipped to [0, 1])
metrics.interpretation: canonical, publication-grade run-relative normalised density derived from canonical_density_v5_adapted. Default plotting metric for Canonical_Metrics.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an absolute density. The normalisation is run-relative; do not compare across different runs unless the normalization reference is identical. NOTE: density_normalized_global is the canonical density max-norm; density_metric_normalized (Density_Metrics sheet) is a DIFFERENT diagnostic descriptor that max-normalises the weighted partial-sum density_metric_raw, not canonical_density_v5_adapted.

metrics.name: density_per_component
metrics.formula: canonical_density_v5_adapted / harmonic_order_count
metrics.interpretation: density normalised by the number of detected harmonic orders.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: energy per partial; the denominator is a count, not a power.

metrics.name: rolloff_compensated_harmonic_density
metrics.formula: harmonic density with α-power frequency rolloff compensation
metrics.interpretation: harmonic density corrected for the spectral envelope rolloff (α exponent).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: comparable across different α values; α is exported alongside as rolloff_compensated_harmonic_density_alpha.

metrics.name: harmonic_effective_power_density
metrics.formula: effective power density of the harmonic template (see density.harmonic_effective_power_density)
metrics.interpretation: summary energy density of the harmonic stack.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: loudness or SPL.

metrics.name: harmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: harmonic_energy_sum / total_component_energy  (mathematically identical to component_harmonic_energy_ratio)
metrics.interpretation: compatibility alias of component_harmonic_energy_ratio. Demoted from canonical → diagnostic in v1.1 of the dictionary because it is the SAME quantity as the explicit canonical name.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent measurement; for new code use component_harmonic_energy_ratio.

metrics.name: inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: inharmonic_energy_sum / total_component_energy  (mathematically identical to component_inharmonic_energy_ratio)
metrics.interpretation: compatibility alias of component_inharmonic_energy_ratio.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent measurement.

metrics.name: subbass_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: subbass_energy_sum / total_component_energy  (mathematically identical to component_subbass_energy_ratio)
metrics.interpretation: compatibility alias of component_subbass_energy_ratio.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a calibrated noise-floor estimate.

metrics.name: harmonic_inharmonic_ratio
metrics.formula: harmonic_energy_sum / inharmonic_energy_sum (linear ratio, not in dB)
metrics.interpretation: linear power ratio between harmonic and inharmonic detected components.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a dB value; convert with 10·log10 if a dB reading is needed.

metrics.name: spectral_entropy
metrics.formula: Shannon entropy of the normalised power spectrum (Σ p_i log p_i, p_i ∝ |X_i|^2)
metrics.interpretation: uniformity of the spectral power distribution; higher → more spread out.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: perceptual brightness or noisiness; entropy and timbre are not the same.

metrics.name: harmonic_completeness
metrics.formula: min(1.0, n_unique_harmonic_orders / max_expected_orders)
metrics.interpretation: fraction of expected integer harmonic orders that were detected.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: raw count of harmonic peaks; this is capped at 1.0 and counts UNIQUE orders only.

metrics.name: f0_final_hz
metrics.formula: final accepted fundamental frequency (Hz) after harmonic fit / policy finalisation in proc_audio
metrics.source: proc_audio (f0_final → main_metrics export); compile_metrics may propagate from per-note row
metrics.interpretation: The analysis-time fundamental used for harmonic indexing, register-dependent low-frequency guards, and related metrics.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: MIDI nominal or filename pitch unless the pipeline explicitly resolved to that value; compare f0_nominal_hz when present.

metrics.name: adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz
metrics.formula: min( max(min_floor_hz, percentage_subfundamental_cutoff_hz, optional leakage_guard_cutoff_hz), f0_final_hz * max_fraction_of_f0 ) — see low_frequency_policy.calculate_adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz
metrics.denominator: n/a (piecewise max then capped vs f0)
metrics.interpretation: Lower-frequency guard: spectral energy below this cutoff in the fixed diagnostic band is classified as subfundamental residual for labelling/audit, not as proven physical sub-bass.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the STFT bin width, the orchestral sub-bass limit, or a noise-floor estimate.

metrics.name: subfundamental_margin_percent
metrics.formula: register-dependent margin M(f0) from low_frequency_policy.calculate_subfundamental_margin_percent (piecewise vs f0 bands)
metrics.interpretation: Nominal register margin below f0 used to form percentage_subfundamental_cutoff_hz = f0 * (1 − M/100).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a loudness cue or psychoacoustic sharpness metric.

metrics.name: percentage_subfundamental_cutoff_hz
metrics.formula: f0_final_hz * (1 − subfundamental_margin_percent / 100)
metrics.interpretation: Pure register-policy cutoff line before min-floor, leakage guard, and f0·max_fraction caps.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the final adaptive guard; use adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz after caps.

metrics.name: leakage_guard_cutoff_hz
metrics.formula: max(0, f0_final_hz − subbass_protection_tolerance_hz) when a finite leakage tolerance (Hz) is available; else NaN
metrics.interpretation: Optional lower bound derived from harmonic main-lobe / protection half-width so window leakage just below f0 is not mis-labelled as musical subfundamental content.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: proof of acoustic leakage level without STFT geometry context.

metrics.name: min_floor_hz
metrics.formula: parameter min_floor_hz to calculate_adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz (default 20 Hz)
metrics.interpretation: Hard lower floor (Hz) in the max-stage of the adaptive cutoff so DC / sub-audible rumble bins do not set an absurdly low guard.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the adaptive cutoff itself.

metrics.name: max_fraction_of_f0
metrics.formula: parameter max_fraction_of_f0 (default 0.95); cap_hz = f0_final_hz * max_fraction_of_f0
metrics.interpretation: Upper cap on the adaptive cutoff as a fraction of f0 so the guard never approaches Nyquist or swallows the fundamental.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the detected bandwidth of the note.

metrics.name: effective_subfundamental_margin_percent
metrics.formula: 100 * (1 − adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz / f0_final_hz)
metrics.source: low_frequency_policy.calculate_adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz; compile_metrics may recompute from stored cutoff
metrics.interpretation: Effective margin (percent of f0) implied by the final adaptive cutoff after all floors/guards/caps.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: identical to subfundamental_margin_percent when caps bind; compare both.

metrics.name: subfundamental_guard_valid
metrics.formula: boolean: finite f0_final_hz > 0 and successful guard computation (see low_frequency_policy._nan_result vs filled result)
metrics.interpretation: True when an adaptive subfundamental guard was computed from a valid f0; false when f0 invalid and policy returns sentinel NaNs.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: evidence that the recording is in tune.

metrics.name: subfundamental_guard_policy
metrics.formula: string policy label from low_frequency_policy (e.g. f0_adaptive_register_margin, invalid_f0)
metrics.interpretation: Documents which guard branch produced the exported cutoff metadata.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: user-facing legal policy text.

metrics.name: low_frequency_policy_version
metrics.formula: constant LOW_FREQUENCY_POLICY_VERSION from low_frequency_policy
metrics.interpretation: Version tag for the register-dependent subfundamental guard and related compile-time fill logic.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an analysis timestamp.

metrics.name: adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_source
metrics.formula: provenance string: e.g. per_note_analysis_export, derived_at_compile_stage_from_f0_final_hz, not_available_missing_f0
metrics.interpretation: Records whether the adaptive cutoff was taken from the per-note analysis export or recomputed at compile from f0_final_hz.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the numeric cutoff value.

metrics.name: physical_low_frequency_lower_hz
metrics.formula: lower edge (Hz) of the fixed diagnostic low-frequency band for physical residual aggregation (default aligns with density.SUBBASS_AGGREGATE_LOWER_HZ, typically 30 Hz)
metrics.interpretation: Lower bound of the Hz window used with physical_low_frequency_upper_hz for fixed-band low-frequency residual bookkeeping.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the adaptive subfundamental guard cutoff.

metrics.name: physical_low_frequency_upper_hz
metrics.formula: upper edge (Hz) of the fixed diagnostic low-frequency band (default aligns with sub-bass aggregate cutoff, typically 200 Hz)
metrics.interpretation: Upper bound of the fixed diagnostic band used with classify_low_frequency_row alongside adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the note's Nyquist frequency.

metrics.name: subfundamental_cutoff_selection_rule
metrics.formula: constant SUBFUNDAMENTAL_CUTOFF_SELECTION_RULE string from low_frequency_policy (human-readable algebra)
metrics.interpretation: Static documentation of how min floor, percentage line, optional leakage guard, and f0·max_fraction interact.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a per-note numeric result.

metrics.name: subfundamental_cutoff_selected_by
metrics.formula: which branch won the max-stage / cap-stage (e.g. min_floor_hz, percentage_subfundamental_cutoff_hz, leakage_guard_cutoff_hz, max_fraction_of_f0_cap, per_note_analysis_export)
metrics.interpretation: Audit field: which input dominated the final adaptive_subfundamental_cutoff_hz after caps.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the cutoff frequency in Hz.

metrics.name: discrete_metric_d3
metrics.formula: discrete density score at d=3 detector level (see density.discrete_density_metrics)
metrics.interpretation: diagnostic discrete-density score using the d=3 detector (legacy parameter sweep). Kept as a numeric feature for exploratory PCA.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the canonical density; refer to canonical_density_v5_adapted.

metrics.name: discrete_metric_d10
metrics.formula: discrete density score at d=10 detector level
metrics.interpretation: diagnostic discrete-density score using the d=10 detector.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the canonical density.

metrics.name: discrete_metric_d17
metrics.formula: discrete density score at d=17 detector level
metrics.interpretation: diagnostic discrete-density score using the d=17 detector.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the canonical density.

metrics.name: discrete_metric_d24
metrics.formula: discrete density score at d=24 detector level
metrics.interpretation: diagnostic discrete-density score using the d=24 detector.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the canonical density.

metrics.name: harmonic_order_count
metrics.formula: count of detected harmonic orders (unique partial indices ≥ 1)
metrics.interpretation: number of detected harmonic orders for the analysed note. Independent of energy distribution.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: energy or amplitude; this is a raw integer count.

metrics.name: harmonic_energy_sum
metrics.formula: Σ harmonic_amps^2
metrics.interpretation: intermediate H used to compute component_harmonic_energy_ratio.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an absolute energy with physical units; STFT amplitude calibration depends on window + n_fft.

metrics.name: inharmonic_energy_sum
metrics.formula: Σ ih_amps_eff^2
metrics.interpretation: intermediate I.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an absolute energy with physical units.

metrics.name: subbass_energy_sum
metrics.formula: aggregate_subbass_noise_peak_power(complete_list_df, harmonic_list_df, …)
metrics.interpretation: intermediate S.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a calibrated dB SPL.

metrics.name: total_component_energy
metrics.formula: H + I + S
metrics.interpretation: denominator audit for the component_* ratios.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: total signal energy; this excludes content outside the analysed peak / sub-bass aggregation.

metrics.name: total_filtered_spectral_energy
metrics.formula: Σ A² over filtered_list_df (peaks surviving noise-floor rejection)
metrics.interpretation: total spectral energy in the filtered (noise-rejected) peak list. Audit/intermediate quantity used to derive the residual diffuse non-harmonic bucket.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the calibrated total signal energy (depends on STFT window and n_fft normalisation).

metrics.name: residual_noise_energy_sum
metrics.formula: max(0, total_filtered_spectral_energy − (H + I + S))
metrics.interpretation: diffuse non-harmonic residual: spectral energy that survived the noise-floor rejection but is NOT in the accepted harmonic peaks, NOT in the discrete inharmonic peaks, and NOT in the sub-bass region.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an alias of inharmonic_energy_sum; this is a separate residual bucket.

metrics.name: component_energy_denominator
metrics.formula: literal string 'H+I+S'
metrics.interpretation: denominator label for the component_* fields; readers should verify this matches their interpretation.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a numeric quantity.

metrics.name: component_energy_method
metrics.formula: literal string 'single_pass_proc_audio_energy'
metrics.interpretation: documents that the component partition was computed by proc_audio's single-pass helper.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a numeric quantity.

metrics.name: component_profile_source
metrics.formula: literal 'integrated_single_pass' or 'legacy_batch'
metrics.interpretation: documents the orchestration mode that produced the component_* fields.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a numeric quantity.

metrics.name: component_energy_quantity
metrics.formula: literal 'power_sum_amplitude_squared'
metrics.interpretation: audit flag asserting that H, I, S were computed as Σ A^2 (power), not Σ |A| (amplitude).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a numeric quantity.

metrics.name: model_weight_denominator
metrics.formula: literal 'harmonic_plus_inharmonic'
metrics.interpretation: documents the H+I denominator of the model_*_weight pair.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a numeric quantity.

metrics.name: n_fft
metrics.formula: STFT frame size requested
metrics.interpretation: FFT length used per frame.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the effective number of analysed bins; see n_fft_effective when zero-padding is in use.

metrics.name: hop_length
metrics.formula: STFT hop length
metrics.interpretation: frame advance for the STFT.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: audio block size for I/O.

metrics.name: snr_threshold_db
metrics.formula: SNR threshold used for peak acceptance
metrics.interpretation: minimum SNR for a spectral peak to be considered a partial.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a multiplicative gain; this is a dB margin added to the local noise floor.

metrics.name: harmonic_tolerance
metrics.formula: tolerance in Hz applied around expected harmonic frequencies
metrics.interpretation: frequency window for harmonic/inharmonic classification.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a perceptual just-noticeable difference; this is an analytical tolerance.

metrics.name: energy_conservation_status
metrics.formula: categorical: 'ok' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'not_available_at_compile_stage'
metrics.interpretation: validation flag for the STFT energy budget.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a numeric quality score.

metrics.name: model_weights_source
metrics.formula: categorical: 'single_pass_proc_audio_energy' | 'apply_filters_arguments' | 'fallback_default' | …
metrics.interpretation: documents where the model_*_weight values came from.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a numeric quantity.

metrics.name: legacy_harmonic_density
metrics.formula: Σ |A_h| (sum of linear amplitudes of harmonic peaks)
metrics.interpretation: deprecated: historical proxy for 'harmonic density'.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: energy or a fraction; this is a raw Σ|A|, NOT Σ A^2 nor a normalised ratio.

metrics.name: legacy_inharmonic_density
metrics.formula: Σ |A_ih|
metrics.interpretation: deprecated: historical proxy.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: energy.

metrics.name: legacy_combined_density
metrics.formula: log-weighted blend of legacy_harmonic_density and legacy_inharmonic_density (see _calculate_metrics)
metrics.interpretation: deprecated: amplitude-based combined density.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an energy ratio or canonical density.

metrics.name: legacy_harmonic_density_percentage
metrics.denominator: Σ|A_h| + Σ|A_ih| (amplitude-sum denominator, NOT H + I + S)
metrics.interpretation: deprecated: 'harmonic percentage' computed on amplitude sums.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the same as component_harmonic_energy_ratio (different denominator AND quantity type).

metrics.name: legacy_inharmonic_density_percentage
metrics.interpretation: deprecated: complement of legacy_harmonic_density_percentage.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the same as component_inharmonic_energy_ratio.

metrics.name: batch_harmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: alias of component_harmonic_energy_ratio (single-pass path); reads from Batch Excel in legacy_batch path
metrics.interpretation: compatibility alias; prefer component_harmonic_energy_ratio in new code.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a separate or independent measurement.

metrics.name: batch_inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: alias of component_inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.interpretation: compatibility alias.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent measurement.

metrics.name: batch_subbass_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: alias of component_subbass_energy_ratio
metrics.interpretation: compatibility alias.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent measurement.

metrics.name: batch_total_inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.formula: alias of component_total_inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.interpretation: compatibility alias.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent measurement.

metrics.name: Harmonic Partials sum
metrics.formula: Σ |A_h| over detected harmonic partials under the per-note weight_function (linear unless overridden)
metrics.interpretation: per-band harmonic density D_H under the compile weight_function (display copy on Density_Metrics as Harmonic Partials sum). For linear weighting, equals harmonic_amplitude_sum; for log/sqrt/d3/etc., follows extract_density_component_sum.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the final density metric. Plot density_metric_normalized (Density_Metrics) or density_normalized_global (Canonical_Metrics) instead.

metrics.name: Inharmonic Partials sum
metrics.formula: Σ |A_i| over detected inharmonic partials under the per-note weight_function
metrics.interpretation: per-band inharmonic density D_I under the compile weight_function (display copy as Inharmonic Partials sum).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the final density metric.

metrics.name: Sub-bass sum
metrics.formula: Σ |A_s| over the sub-bass aggregation under the per-note weight_function
metrics.interpretation: per-band sub-bass density D_S under the compile weight_function (display copy as Sub-bass sum).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the final density metric.

metrics.name: Total sum
metrics.formula: Harmonic Partials sum + Inharmonic Partials sum + Sub-bass sum
metrics.source: proc_audio Metrics export (partial_metric_sums_h_i_s_total) — also recomputed by compile_metrics from the per-band sums
metrics.interpretation: unweighted diagnostic total of the three per-band partial sums.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: the final density metric; it ignores the canonical component_*_energy_ratio weighting. Plot density_metric_normalized (Density_Metrics) or density_normalized_global (Canonical_Metrics) instead.

metrics.name: weighted_harmonic_density_contribution
metrics.formula: D_H * w_H = harmonic_density_sum * component_harmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.unit: same scale as Harmonic Partials sum (unbounded)
metrics.interpretation: harmonic-band contribution to density_metric_raw under the single-pass component energy weighting.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent canonical descriptor; it is an additive term of density_metric_raw and shares the latter's run-relative interpretation. NOT for publication.

metrics.name: weighted_inharmonic_density_contribution
metrics.formula: D_I * w_I = inharmonic_density_sum * component_inharmonic_energy_ratio
metrics.unit: same scale as Inharmonic Partials sum (unbounded)
metrics.interpretation: inharmonic-band contribution to density_metric_raw under the single-pass component energy weighting.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent canonical descriptor; additive term of density_metric_raw.

metrics.name: weighted_subbass_density_contribution
metrics.formula: D_S * w_S = subbass_density_sum * component_subbass_energy_ratio
metrics.interpretation: sub-bass-band contribution to density_metric_raw under the single-pass component energy weighting.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: an independent canonical descriptor; additive term of density_metric_raw.

metrics.name: density_weighted_sum
metrics.formula: harmonic_density_sum * w_H + inharmonic_density_sum * w_I + subbass_density_sum * w_S
metrics.unit: depends on compile weight_function (linear ~ amplitude scale; log ~ log-density scale)
metrics.interpretation: Stage-2 weighted note density using per-band D values under the compile weight_function and measured component energy ratios. Numerically equal to density_metric_raw when extraction status is ok.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a linear sum of Amplitude_raw (see harmonic_amplitude_sum). Not invariant to weight_function changes. Not a canonical publication metric — prefer effective_partial_density on Canonical_Metrics.

metrics.name: density_log_weighted
metrics.formula: log10(1 + density_weighted_sum)
metrics.interpretation: log-space view of density_weighted_sum. Default auto-plot preference on Density_Metrics sheet when present (publication_chart_policy).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: independent of compile weight_function; it inherits sensitivity from density_weighted_sum.

metrics.name: density_metric_raw
metrics.formula: D_H * w_H + D_I * w_I + D_S * w_S where D_H,D_I,D_S are per-band densities from extract_density_component_sum under the compile weight_function; w_* are component_*_energy_ratio
metrics.unit: same scale as the per-band partial sums (unbounded)
metrics.interpretation: audit-grade intermediate value: equals density_weighted_sum. Reflects the compile weight_function. Useful for verification; cross-run comparison requires noting the weight_function key.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a publication-grade density metric. Plot density_metric_normalized instead. Unbounded; not normalized; not comparable across runs.

metrics.name: density_metric_normalized
metrics.formula: density_metric_raw / max(density_metric_raw over the current compiled workbook)
metrics.unit: dimensionless (0..1) within the current run only
metrics.interpretation: Density_Metrics-sheet default plotting metric. Run-relative normalization of density_metric_raw to [0, 1]; suitable for comparison *within* one compiled workbook. The canonical, corpus-wide normalized density descriptor for publication is density_normalized_global (Canonical_Metrics, derived from canonical_density_v5_adapted).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: comparable across different runs unless the normalization reference (max(density_metric_raw)) is identical. Not a canonical publication metric — prefer density_normalized_global for cross-run publication. Run-relative normalization; do not compare across different runs unless the normalization reference is identical. NOTE: previous versions aliased density_metric_normalized to density_normalized_global; the two are now distinct descriptors.

metrics.name: Combined Density Metric
metrics.formula: Stage-1 combined_density_metric_value (log/expm1 or calculate_combined_density_metric on harmonic + inharmonic legacy scalars)
metrics.interpretation: v5-style combined harmonic/inharmonic density scalar. Stronger dynamic separation than density_weighted_sum under log compile on some corpora.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: equal to density_weighted_sum or the research mean (DWS+CDM)/2; not on Density_Metrics allow-list.

metrics.name: Spectral Density Metric
metrics.formula: spectral_density_metric_value (whole-spectrum legacy path; masking off when spectral_masking_enabled=False)
metrics.interpretation: Legacy whole-spectrum density input to Weighted Combined Metric (harmonic_col at compile).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: canonical_density or effective_partial_density.

metrics.name: Filtered Density Metric
metrics.formula: filtered_density_metric_value = apply_density_metric on filtered_list_df amplitudes
metrics.interpretation: Legacy inharmonic/filtered-list density input to Weighted Combined Metric (inharmonic_col at compile).
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: inharmonic_density_sum on Density_Metrics (different construction).

metrics.name: Weighted Combined Metric
metrics.formula: f(alpha * SDM + beta * FDM) with f from compile weight_function (e.g. log -> log1p)
metrics.interpretation: v5-style weighted legacy combination using model_harmonic_weight / model_inharmonic_weight (H/(H+I)), not measured H+I+S energy ratios.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: density_weighted_sum_cdm_mean; forbidden on Density_Metrics sheet.

metrics.name: density_weighted_sum_cdm_mean
metrics.formula: (density_weighted_sum + Combined Density Metric) / 2
metrics.unit: mixed scale (editorial average of two unlike definitions)
metrics.interpretation: Research-workbook-only editorial blend for plotting when a single column is desired. Not computed at compile time.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a canonical acoustic measure, Weighted Combined Metric, or energy-weighted density.

metrics.name: compilation_error
metrics.formula: n/a — placeholder column emitted when no exportable metrics are available
metrics.interpretation: indicates that the requested compilation failed or had no data; investigate logs.
metrics.do_not_interpret_as: a metric. This column MUST NEVER appear in Canonical_Metrics.
